metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state the expand-contract or backward-compatibility strategy for `load_criteria`, `check_registry_coverage`, and `plan_review_assemble_criteria` (e.g. 'add `repo_root=None` default; None → packaged-only behaviour preserved'), enumerate all call sites that must be updated, and document a rollback path (e.g. revert to `repo_root=None` default).
findings.finding: The plan changes existing function signatures (`load_criteria`, `check_registry_coverage`, `plan_review_assemble_criteria`) to accept `repo_root` but provides no expand-contract sequencing, default-parameter fallback strategy, or rollback path for existing callers of these functions.
findings.location: What section — 'route it through every plan-review vocabulary site: load_criteria, check_registry_coverage, and the workflow Pass-1 batch vocab in workflow_ops'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a compatibility note in What: 'Add repo_root=None with packaged-only fallback to preserve existing callers; enumerate all call sites in registry.py, workflow_ops.py and tests; rollback = revert parameter default.' Then add a Scope line listing any additional files containing calls to these functions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism by which the workflow YAML gains a batch step (or equivalent dispatch) for each activated project criterion — e.g., dynamic fan-out via a criteria-list output + a single parameterized batch step, or a workflow-YAML regeneration/patch step triggered at activation time — so that `include_project_<name>=True` from `assemble_criteria` actually causes the criterion to run in Pass-1.
findings.finding: The plan's mechanism for routing activated project criteria through the workflow Pass-1 batch vocabulary is incomplete: `plan_review_assemble_criteria` emits `include_<id>` booleans that the batch step's `when:` predicates consume, but those `when:` clauses reference STATICALLY NAMED output slots in the workflow YAML. A project criterion activated at runtime produces a new `include_project_myname` key in the step output, but no corresponding batch step with `when: ${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_project_myname }}` will exist in the workflow YAML unless it is regenerated or patched. The plan says 'project ids must enter it too, else an activated criterion never fans out' — correctly naming the problem — but does not specify the mechanism by which the workflow YAML gains the required batch step.
findings.location: What section / AC-1 ('an activated project LLM criterion fans out and runs in plan-review Pass-1') / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:154-165 / src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml
findings.suggested_fix: Consider replacing the static per-criterion `include_<ID>` fan-out with a single `criteria_list` output (a JSON array of criterion ids to run) consumed by a parameterized batch step that iterates it. This avoids the static-vocab problem entirely for both current and future project criteria, and removes the need to patch the workflow YAML at criterion-activation time. If the workflow engine requires static `when:` slots, add a 'workflow YAML regeneration' step (analogous to `criteria_routing.json` derivation) to the activation flow and document it in the ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the static `plan-review.yaml` batch `criteria:` vocabulary is extended for project ids (e.g., schema-level dynamic batch directive, code-generated YAML regeneration, or a runtime batch override in the workflow executor), so an activated `project.<name>` criterion actually fans out in Pass-1.
findings.finding: The plan says 'project ids must enter [the workflow Pass-1 batch vocabulary] too,' but the batch criteria list in `plan-review.yaml` is a STATIC, committed YAML artifact with hardcoded `when: ${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_<ID> }}` entries only for `CANONICAL_LLM` ids. A project criterion emitting `include_project_my_rule: bool` from `plan_review_assemble_criteria` will never be consumed by any batch `when:` clause — the YAML has no entry for it. The plan does not specify how this static vocabulary is extended (generated, runtime-injected, or a dynamic batch directive) to admit project ids.
findings.location: § What — 'workflow Pass-1 batch vocab in workflow_ops (plan_review_assemble_criteria)'; AC1 — 'an activated project LLM criterion fans out and runs in plan-review Pass-1'; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml lines 55–117

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and implement the mechanism by which a project criterion's `{prompt, when}` entry enters the Pass-1 batch: either (a) dynamically inject activated project criteria into the batch step's criteria list at run time (requires interpreter/runner changes), or (b) define a workflow-generation step that regenerates `plan-review.yaml` when the overlay changes (and document the re-generate-on-overlay-change contract), or (c) use a different fan-out path (e.g., a separate `uses` step per project criterion) — whichever path is chosen must be grounded in the interpreter's actual execution model.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that an activated project LLM criterion 'fans out and runs in plan-review Pass-1' (AC line 1, MVP end-to-end), but the v3 batch step in `plan-review.yaml` has a static, committed `criteria:` list — each entry is a hardcoded `{prompt: plan-review-<ID>, when: '${{ steps.assemble.outputs.include_<ID> }}'}` pair. A project criterion with id `project.my_crit` cannot fan out unless a matching entry exists in the YAML (or the batch step gains a dynamic criteria-injection capability). The plan doesn't specify which mechanism is used, leaving a real capability gap between the assemble op emitting `include_project_my_crit: True` and the interpreter actually executing that criterion.
findings.location: AC #1 ('an activated project LLM criterion fans out and runs in plan-review Pass-1') + 'Scope / MVP end-to-end' section + src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (static `batch.criteria` list) + src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:154-165 (canonical list drives output keys)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a caller-acknowledgment note to the plan: enumerate the current call sites of `load_criteria` and `check_registry_coverage`, specify the new signature (e.g., `repo_root: Path | None = None`), and state the fallback behavior when `repo_root` is absent (packaged-only, no overlay).
findings.finding: The plan routes `load_criteria` and `check_registry_coverage` through `effective_criteria(repo_root)`, which implies adding a `repo_root` parameter to these functions (or threading it through their call sites), but the plan does not acknowledge existing callers that invoke them today without a `repo_root` argument, nor does it specify the migration strategy (default=None? overloaded? caller audit?).
findings.location: ## What — first bullet; ## Acceptance Criteria — AC1
findings.suggested_fix: In the ## What section, add: 'Signature change: `load_criteria(repo_root: Path | None = None)` and `check_registry_coverage(repo_root: Path | None = None)`; when `repo_root` is None, behavior is identical to today (packaged index only, no overlay). Audit existing call sites in `workflow_ops.py` and any tests — pass `repo_root` where available, else leave None to preserve backward compatibility.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the cache-key strategy to a single decision (e.g. 'content hash of overlay file bytes, falling back to uncached if file absent') so the executor has no fork to choose from.
findings.finding: The cache strategy for `effective_routing(repo_root)` is left as an unresolved fork: 'uncached, or keyed by (repo_root, overlay mtime) — note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky.' An implementing agent must choose between two approaches without a clear default decision, making this a deferral of a real design choice to the executor.
findings.location: What section — Cache isolation paragraph; also AC #4
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the 'or' clause with: 'keyed by `(repo_root, sha256(overlay_bytes))` where overlay_bytes is read once at call time; if the overlay file is absent the key is `(repo_root, None)`.' This eliminates mtime granularity risk and satisfies AC4's cross-repo leakage guarantee deterministically.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the cache isolation approach with AC4's determinism requirement by committing to content-hash keying in the What section so the two sections are no longer in tension.
findings.finding: The What section's cache isolation note implicitly acknowledges that mtime-keyed caching may be unreliable ('note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky'), yet AC4 requires a deterministic RED test proving 'no cross-repo routing leakage in the long-lived MCP server.' If an executor picks the mtime branch, AC4 may pass or fail non-deterministically — the approach section and the acceptance criterion are in tension.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to content-hash (read + SHA-256 the overlay file) as the DEFAULT cache key for `effective_routing(repo_root)`, reserving mtime only as a fast pre-check (compare mtime first; if changed, re-read and hash); document this in the implementation note so the RED test is meaningful on all target filesystems.
findings.finding: The plan leaves the mtime-vs-content-hash choice for `effective_routing(repo_root)` caching unresolved ('note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky'), but does not specify a decision criterion or a default. In the long-lived MCP server (the primary scenario the RED test targets), sub-second writes on filesystems with 1-second mtime granularity (common in Docker/CI) can produce a stale cache hit even after a content change — the very cross-repo leakage the RED test is designed to catch. The plan correctly diagnoses the risk but defers the decision without a binding default.
findings.location: What section / Cache isolation paragraph / AC-4 ('Overlay merge is cache-isolated per repo')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to one cache strategy for `effective_routing(repo_root)` — either content-hash-of-overlay-file (recommended: immune to mtime granularity) or explicit 'uncached' with a documented performance note — and state it as the authoritative choice rather than leaving 'prefer content hash if flaky' as a conditional.
findings.finding: The cache-key strategy for `effective_routing(repo_root)` is given as '`(repo_root, overlay mtime)` — note mtime granularity, prefer content hash if flaky' without defining when 'flaky' triggers the switch to content hash, nor who makes that call. This leaves the cache invalidation protocol ambiguous: an mtime-keyed implementation won't catch same-second writes (e.g. in tests); a content-hash implementation has a different cost profile and requires reading the file on every call.
findings.location: What section ('Cache isolation'); AC line 4 ('Overlay merge is cache-isolated per repo')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the parenthetical with: 'Cache `effective_routing(repo_root)` keyed by `(repo_root, sha256(overlay_bytes))` — read the file once, hash the bytes, use as the cache key. If the overlay is absent, treat as empty (cache the empty merge). This is immune to mtime granularity and subsumes the mtime option.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC 7 to require at minimum a live (non-mocked) LLM invocation for the activated project criterion, or explicitly name a child ticket where the live end-to-end test is deferred with a documented rationale for why the offline substitute is faithful.
findings.finding: AC 7 ('activate one criterion → runs → surfaces a finding') does not specify whether the end-to-end demo must exercise a live LLM call or may be satisfied by a mock/offline substitute. Since this is a new defaulted code path (project criterion routed through Pass-1 for the first time), validating it only through a canned-response fake would be a proxy-validation anti-pattern: the suite goes green while the live path could be broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC 7: 'MVP slice demonstrated end-to-end (activate one criterion → runs → surfaces a finding)'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC 7 to: 'MVP slice demonstrated end-to-end with a live LLM call (not a canned response): activate one project criterion → it is assembled into the Pass-1 batch prompt → a real LLM response is parsed → a finding is surfaced. An integration test tagged @live or equivalent must gate this AC.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the canonical sanitization function (e.g. `def _key_for_id(cid: str) -> str: return cid.replace('.', '_')`) as the single source of truth, explicitly referenced by both the assemble op and the workflow YAML generation/consume site, and add a round-trip unit test.
findings.finding: The `.`→`_` sanitization rule is stated for the `include_<id>` output key at the assemble site, but the INVERSE mapping — how the consume site (workflow `when:` expression or downstream op) recovers `project.<name>` from `include_project_<name>` — is not specified. Without a canonical round-trip rule, the assemble and consume sites can independently implement incompatible sanitizations.
findings.location: What section ('keep the rule co-located'); AC line 3 ('include_<id> key sanitization applied at both assemble + consume sites'); workflow_ops.py:165

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit load-time fallback protocol for an activated project criterion whose prompt file is absent: either (a) emit a located `RegistryError` with the missing file path (strict) or (b) skip the criterion with a warning and record it in coverage as `skipped_missing_prompt: [ids]` (fail-open); specify which posture this story adopts.
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the case where a project criterion is listed in `activate` but its prompt file is absent or malformed. The plan specifies load-time 'located error' for namespace collisions/rebinds but is silent on missing-prompt for a validly-namespaced activated criterion — leaving the caller (workflow op, gate runner) without a defined error-vs-skip protocol.
findings.location: What section ('.rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay'); AC line 2 ('load-time collision/rebind rejection (located error)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the ADR file path (e.g. `docs/adr/NNNN-criteria-overlay.md`) and a brief description of required content (overlay merge rules, activation contract, collision rejection rationale) to the Scope file list and What section so AC6 has a traceable deliverable.
findings.finding: AC6 (ADR) is an orphan deliverable: the plan body (What, Scope, file list) never describes what the ADR should contain, where it lives, or who is responsible for it — the Scope file list omits any ADR path entirely.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6; Scope — Files list
findings.suggested_fix: In Scope → Files, add `docs/adr/NNNN-unified-registry-overlay.md`; in What, add one sentence: 'Write an ADR at that path covering: overlay merge algorithm, activation semantics, collision/rebind rejection rationale, and cache isolation strategy.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the descriptor branch for `exec: DET` project criteria in `_descriptor_from_prompt` (skip `get_prompt`, build descriptor from routing `name`/`scenario`/`facet`) AND clarify how a `project.<name>` prompt id maps to a filename that survives `_user_prompt_ids`'s dot-filter (line 106 of prompt_library.py).
findings.finding: `effective_criteria(repo_root)` return type and descriptor shape for `exec: DET` project criteria is undefined in this story's scope, yet `_descriptor_from_prompt` (called by both `load_criteria` and `check_registry_coverage`) unconditionally invokes `get_prompt(...)` and raises `RegistryError` if the prompt is absent — so any activated DET criterion will crash the load path under the current architecture.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph 'DET branch' to the What section: 'For `exec: DET` project criteria, `_descriptor_from_prompt` skips `get_prompt`; `name` = routing `name` or id; `scenario` = routing `description`; `facet` = routing `facet` or id; `checklist` = []. For LLM project criteria the prompt file is `plan-review-<sanitized-id>.md` where dots in the id are replaced with hyphens before the filename lookup.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the overlay file is read-once atomically (open → read → hash/stat in one syscall sequence) before cache lookup/store, and document whether a lock or copy-on-read strategy is used to prevent TOCTOU in the long-lived MCP server.
findings.finding: The `effective_routing(repo_root)` cache strategy is noted as mtime-keyed with acknowledged flakiness, but the plan does not specify how concurrent requests in the long-lived MCP server atomically read the overlay file and populate the cache — creating a TOCTOU window where mtime is checked, the file changes, and stale content is cached.
findings.location: Cache isolation section / AC: 'Overlay merge is cache-isolated per repo'
findings.suggested_fix: Read the file contents once, compute the content hash from those bytes, and use (repo_root, content_hash) as the cache key — all within a single open() call — so the key and the value are always consistent. Document this contract in the ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the RETIRE/eviction policy for `effective_routing` cache entries: either use `maxsize` on lru_cache, a per-key invalidation strategy, or explicitly justify 'uncached' (recompute every call) as the safe default.
findings.finding: If `effective_routing` is cached with a `(repo_root, mtime)` or `(repo_root, content_hash)` lru_cache key, old cache entries for prior mtime/hash values accumulate unboundedly in a long-lived MCP server — there is no RETIRE/eviction path defined for stale entries.
findings.location: Cache isolation section / 'effective_routing(repo_root) (uncached, or keyed by (repo_root, overlay mtime))'
findings.suggested_fix: Default to the simplest safe approach: make `effective_routing` uncached (compute on every call, since overlay reads are rare and cheap), and only introduce keyed caching with an explicit maxsize and invalidation story if profiling shows it necessary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split the CI drift/parity gate (AC 5 second clause) and the ADR (AC 6) into a follow-on ticket so this leaf story focuses on: overlay loading, `effective_criteria`, cache isolation, and the MVP end-to-end demo (ACs 1–4 + 7).
findings.finding: The ticket carries 7 acceptance criteria and joins at least two independent sub-goals — the CI drift/parity gate (a CI infrastructure concern) and the ADR documentation — alongside the core vocabulary-opening work. These are separable deliverables that can succeed or fail independently of the registry/overlay logic, pushing the ticket over the >6 AC threshold and the single-concern test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all 7 items); 'Routing validation' bullet in What section
findings.suggested_fix: Create a child/follow-on ticket 'Registry overlay: CI drift gate + ADR' containing AC 5 (parity gate) and AC 6 (ADR). Keep this ticket at ACs 1–4 + 7, which form a coherent vertical slice.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
